text.title: Caching Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every caching-strategy artefact must obey. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Cache pattern MUST match read/write ratio: cache-aside for >10:1 reads; read-through for >5:1; write-through for write-heavy < stale-tolerance 1s; write-behind for high-write-tolerance loss-acceptable workloads.

rationale.source: Stack Overflow Architecture + AWS caching whitepaper

rule.rationale: Wrong pattern doubles either staleness or write latency; matching the pattern to ratio is the largest single decision.

rule.statement: Per-key TTL MUST include random jitter (e.g. base ± 20%) to prevent synchronised expiry stampedes.

rationale.source: AWS caching whitepaper + Internal — outage post-mortems 2024

rule.rationale: Without jitter, popular keys expire at the same instant; cache rebuild creates a thundering herd that overloads the source.

rule.statement: Cache MUST declare an explicit invalidation contract: TTL-only OR write-through OR pub/sub event. "Hope it expires soon" is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without a contract, writers in other services have no path to flush the cache; stale data persists past SLA.

rule.statement: On cache miss, only ONE coroutine MUST recompute per key; concurrent requests for the same key MUST wait for the in-flight one. Thundering-herd patterns are forbidden.

rationale.source: Russ Cox — Singleflight + Internal — incident post-mortems

rule.rationale: Without single-flight, N concurrent misses launch N source queries; the source dies under the load it should be cached from.

rule.statement: Hit-rate target MUST be >= 0.80 for cache-aside on stable hot keys; an alert MUST fire when 5-minute rate drops below the floor.
rule.rationale: Hit-rate is the only signal that catches a broken cache before latency degrades; without an alert, broken caches go unnoticed for days.
